summary: PR 2453 focused on decomp/matching work in src/melee/ty/toy.c with small signature/type adjustments in toy.h and gm_1601.c. It implemented un_803067BC, improved several toy functions, converted multiple OSReport plus __assert pairs to HSD_ASSERTREPORT, adjusted some signatures and extern/literal usage, and the bot report recorded four new matches plus one broken match and several regressions.

key_files.role: Primary decomp/matching edits: implemented un_803067BC; adjusted un_80306D70, un_80306EEC, un_80307018, un_803075E8, un_8030813C, un_80308354, un_80309338, and un_803102D0; added debug/stddef includes and several extern declarations.
evidence_refs: changed_files.jsonl: src/melee/ty/toy.c added 116 deleted 49 hunks 16
raw_diff: src/melee/ty/toy.c hunks
text_corpus: decomp-dev bot report for main/melee/ty/toy

key_files.role: Header update for un_8030813C signature from s16 to s32 and removal of local extern declarations for un_80308354-related strings.
evidence_refs: changed_files.jsonl: src/melee/ty/toy.h added 1 deleted 6 hunks 2
raw_diff: toy.h un_8030813C prototype and extern removals

key_files.role: Small type adjustments in gm_801604DC and gm_80160564 locals from s16 to s32.
evidence_refs: changed_files.jsonl: src/melee/gm/gm_1601.c added 2 deleted 2 hunks 2
raw_diff: gm_1601.c var_r3 type changes

what_changed.text: Implemented un_803067BC in toy.c, replacing a decomp stub marker with C code that copies s16 trophy/model indices through un_804D6E64 and un_804D6EDC using forward and reverse/strided loops.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: toy.c - /// #un_803067BC replaced by function body
text_corpus: bot report lists un_803067BC improvement from 0.00% to 82.64%

what_changed.text: Converted several OSReport followed by __assert patterns in toy.c to HSD_ASSERTREPORT calls with line numbers such as 0x8CD, 0x912, 0xA41, 0xA75, 0xBA3, and 0xC2A.
Changed un_8030813C argument type from s16 to s32 in both toy.c and toy.h.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: toy.c char* un_8030813C(s32 arg0, enum_t unused)
raw_diff: toy.h prototype changed to s32 arg0

what_changed.text: Adjusted gm_801604DC and gm_80160564 locals var_r3 from s16 to s32.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: gm_1601.c two var_r3 type changes

what_changed.text: Changed un_80306EEC to call HSD_LObjReqAnimAll with un_804DDCD8 instead of literal 0.0F, check position_anim instead of next, and use HSD_LObjSetNext(prev, lobj) instead of direct next assignment plus dead fake-match assert code.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: toy.c un_80306EEC hunk

what_changed.text: Adjusted un_80309338 expression ordering and float/double constants to use extern symbols un_804DDCD8, un_804DDD88, and un_804DDD90; bot report says it reached 100.00%.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: toy.c un_80309338 hunk
text_corpus: bot report lists un_80309338 from 99.92% to 100.00%

what_changed.text: Adjusted un_803102D0 to load string addresses from un_803FDD18 plus offsets instead of named string symbols; bot report says it reached 100.00%.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: toy.c un_803102D0 hunk
text_corpus: bot report lists un_803102D0 from 72.38% to 100.00%

smart_moves.text: Replacing an explicit fake-match dead-code assert in un_80306EEC with HSD_LObjSetNext(prev, lobj) made the source more idiomatic and aligned with header/helper cleanup, while still slightly improving that function according to the bot report.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: toy.c removed comment 'FAKE MATCH: Dead code needed for binary match' and direct prev->next assignment
raw_diff: toy.c added HSD_LObjSetNext(prev, lobj)
text_corpus: bot report lists un_80306EEC 96.39% to 96.40%

smart_moves.text: The PR kept un_8030813C prototype and header declaration synchronized after widening arg0 from s16 to s32.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: toy.c un_8030813C signature
raw_diff: toy.h un_8030813C prototype

decomp_lessons.lesson: When an OSReport immediately precedes a plain assert in toy.c, HSD_ASSERTREPORT may be the likely source-level form if the line number and report arguments can be preserved.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: multiple OSReport + __assert pairs replaced with HSD_ASSERTREPORT

decomp_lessons.lesson: Helper calls can remove fake-match artifacts: in un_80306EEC, HSD_LObjSetNext(prev, lobj) replaced a direct next write plus deliberately unreachable assert block.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: un_80306EEC fake-match block removed
raw_diff: un_80306EEC HSD_LObjSetNext added

decomp_lessons.lesson: Header prototypes should be updated when matching work changes a recovered function parameter type, as shown by un_8030813C changing from s16 to s32 in both toy.c and toy.h.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: toy.c and toy.h un_8030813C signature changes

naming_conventions.text: The PR largely retained address-style names for uncertain toy symbols and functions, such as un_803067BC, un_8030813C, un_804D6EDC, and un_804DDCD8, rather than inventing semantic names.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: toy.c address-style symbol names throughout changed hunks

naming_conventions.text: Locals remain mostly register/decompiler-style names such as var_r29, var_r30, temp_r0_3, and temp_f4 where semantics were not established by the PR evidence.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: un_803067BC and un_80309338 local declarations

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: un_803067BC uses manual pointer arithmetic and an unrolled reverse-copy block of eight s16 stores followed by a remainder loop; the bot report shows it improved from 0.00% to 82.64%, but the PR evidence does not show that natural loop alternatives were tested.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: un_803067BC copied eight values per loop using var_r5[0], var_r5[-1] ... var_r5[-7]
text_corpus: bot report un_803067BC 0.00% to 82.64%

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: un_80308354 uses register s32 target and explicit goto labels around an HSD_ASSERTREPORT path; the bot report says the function became a 100.00% match.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: un_80308354 register target and goto labels
text_corpus: bot report un_80308354 68.06% to 100.00%

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: un_8030813C contains an empty conditional expression `if ((arg0 && arg0) && arg0) {}` as a matching tactic; the bot report says the function became a 100.00% match, but the source shape is not explained by comments or review.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: un_8030813C empty if block
text_corpus: bot report un_8030813C 95.72% to 100.00%

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: un_80309338 changed arithmetic ordering and used extern floating constants for 0.0, 0.5, and 3.0 equivalents; this matched the function per bot report but also coincided with .sdata2 regression noise.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: un_80309338 uses un_804DDCD8, un_804DDD88, un_804DDD90
text_corpus: bot report un_80309338 99.92% to 100.00% and .sdata2 regression

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: un_803102D0 replaced named string symbols with base-plus-offset accesses from un_803FDD18; this improved the function to 100.00% per bot report, but the PR evidence also reports .sdata/.sdata2 regressions and gives no data-ownership rationale.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: un_803102D0 temp_r3 = un_803FDD18 and offset string arguments
text_corpus: bot report un_803102D0 72.38% to 100.00%, .sdata and .sdata2 regressions

accepted_candidate_records.title: Use existing HSD report/assert macro when OSReport plus assert pair matches it
accepted_candidate_records.text: In toy.c PR 2453, multiple OSReport followed by __assert blocks were replaced by HSD_ASSERTREPORT while preserving line numbers and report text/arguments. This is consistent with assert/report macro cleanup when local evidence identifies the macro form.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: un_80306D70, un_80307018, un_803075E8, un_8030813C, un_80308354 HSD_ASSERTREPORT replacements

accepted_candidate_records.title: Prefer recovered helper calls over fake-match direct field writes
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2453 removed a commented fake-match dead-code assert around prev->next and used HSD_LObjSetNext(prev, lobj) in un_80306EEC. This is a localized example of replacing fake-match artifacts with an existing helper.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: un_80306EEC fake-match block removed and HSD_LObjSetNext added

source_update_candidates.title: PR 2453 toy.c matching outcomes and regressions
source_update_candidates.text: For src/melee/ty/toy.c, decomp-dev bot reported four new 100% matches in un_80308354, un_803102D0, un_8030813C, and un_80309338; one broken match in un_80308250; improvements in un_803067BC and un_80306EEC; and regressions in un_803075E8, .sdata2, and .sdata.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: decomp-dev bot report
source_update_candidates.reason: This is a path-specific historical matching/regression fact from the PR bot report; curator should decide whether to store it as a path fact.

source_update_candidates.title: toy.h un_8030813C parameter widened to s32 in PR 2453
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2453 changed un_8030813C's first parameter from s16 to s32 in both src/melee/ty/toy.c and src/melee/ty/toy.h.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: toy.c and toy.h signature changes
source_update_candidates.reason: Signature update is concrete and path-specific, but should be curated against current headers/source before promotion.

rejection_notes: Do not promote the empty `if ((arg0 && arg0) && arg0) {}` in un_8030813C as a general tactic; the diff shows it and the bot reports a match, but no rationale or review justification is present.

Do not promote string-symbol-to-base-offset changes in un_803102D0 or extern float constant use in un_80309338 as accepted style; the bot report also shows .sdata/.sdata2 regressions and the PR body gives no data-ownership explanation.
Do not infer human reviewer approval or rationale; the loaded slice has no human review comments.
